task_completion.description: Access the ManageEngine EventLog Analyzer web console
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open displaying the ManageEngine EventLog Analyzer interface.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the IP restriction settings
task_completion.visual_evidence: The settings page for 'Trusted Host' or 'IP Access Control' (or similar IP restriction feature) is visible in the EventLog Analyzer interface.
The IP address '127.0.0.1' is visible in the list of allowed IPs or currently being entered into the allowed IP input field.
The IP address '10.20.30.40' is visible in the list of allowed IPs or currently being entered into the allowed IP input field.

task_completion.description: Ensure ONLY the two specified IPs are allowed and settings are saved
task_completion.visual_evidence: The final configuration list shows exactly '127.0.0.1' and '10.20.30.40' with no other IP addresses present, and there is evidence the settings have been saved or applied (e.g., a success message or a finalized list view).

integrity.description: The agent used the web console GUI to configure the settings
integrity.visual_evidence: The configuration is performed within a web browser displaying the EventLog Analyzer UI, rather than using terminal commands or editing configuration files directly in a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual ManageEngine application
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show genuine navigation and interaction with the EventLog Analyzer menus and forms, not a mock-up, local HTML file, or text document.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the configuration
integrity.visual_evidence: Browser developer tools (Inspect Element) are not open or being used to alter the text on the screen to fake the presence of the IP addresses.
